1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: SAY: "I HAVE been informed that a number of jinns had listened; then said: 'We have heard the wondrous Qur'an,

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “I have received the divine revelation that some jinns attentively listened to my recitation, so they said, ‘We have heard a unique Qur’an.’

1.author: Arberry
1.text: Say: 'It has been revealed to me that a company of the jinn gave ear, then they said, "We have indeed heard a Koran wonderful,

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: Say thou: it hath been revealed Unto me that a company of the Jinn listened, and said, verily we have listened to a Recitation wondrous.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "It has been revealed to me that a group (from three to ten in number) of jinns listened (to this Quran). They said: 'Verily! We have heard a wonderful Recital (this Quran)!

1.author: Itani
1.text: Say, “It was revealed to me that a band of jinn listened in, and said, ‘We have heard a wondrous Quran.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: Say, (O Prophet), it was revealed to me that a band of jinn attentively listened to (the recitation of the Qur'an) and then (went back to their people) and said:

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: Say: "It has been revealed to me that a group of Jinn listened. They said: `Verily, we have heard a wonderful Recitation!'

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: Say (O Muhammad): It is revealed unto me that a company of the Jinn gave ear, and they said: Lo! we have heard a marvellous Qur'an,

1.author: Qarai
1.text: Say, ‘It has been revealed to me that a team of the jinn listened [to the Quran] and they said, “Indeed we heard a wonderful Quran,

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: Say: 'It is revealed to me that a party of jinn listened then said: "We have indeed heard a wonderful Koran,

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: Say, [O Muhammad], "It has been revealed to me that a group of the jinn listened and said, 'Indeed, we have heard an amazing Qur'an.

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: (Muhammad), say, "It has been revealed to me that a party of jinn has listened (to the recitation) of the Quran and has told (their people), "We heard an amazing reading

1.author: Shakir
1.text: Say: It has been revealed to me that a party of the jinn listened, and they said: Surely we have heard a wonderful Quran,

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: Say, "It has been revealed to me that a band of the jinn listened [to the Quran] and they said, "We have heard a really wonderful recital,

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: Say: It has been revealed to me that a company of Jinns listened (to the Qur'an). They said, 'We have really heard a wonderful Recital!

1.author: Transliteration
1.text: Qul oohiya ilayya annahu istamaAAa nafarun mina aljinni faqaloo inna samiAAna quranan AAajaban

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: Which guides to the right path; and we have come to believe in it, and will not associate any one with our Lord.

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: ‘That guides to the path of goodness, we have therefore accepted faith in it; and we shall never ascribe anyone as a partner to our Lord.’

2.author: Arberry
2.text: guiding to rectitude. We believe in it, and we will not associate with our Lord anyone.

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: Guiding Unto rectitude; wherefore we have believed therein, and we shall by no means associate with our Lord anyone.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: 'It guides to the Right Path, and we have believed therein, and we shall never join (in worship) anything with our Lord (Allah).

2.author: Itani
2.text: It guides to rectitude, so we have believed in it; and we will never associate anyone with our Lord.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: “We have indeed heard a wonderful Qur'an which guides to the Right Way; so we have come to believe in it, and we will not associate aught with Our Lord in His Divinity”;

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: `It guides to the right path, and we have believed therein, and we shall never join anything with our Lord.'

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: Which guideth unto righteousness, so we believe in it and we ascribe no partner unto our Lord.

2.author: Qarai
2.text: which guides to rectitude. Hence, we have believed in it and we will never ascribe any partner to our Lord.

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: that guides to the Right Path. We believe in it and we will not associate anyone with our Lord.

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: It guides to the right course, and we have believed in it. And we will never associate with our Lord anyone.

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: which guides people to the right path and we believe in it. We shall never consider anyone equal to our Lord;

2.author: Shakir
2.text: Guiding to the right way, so we believe in it, and we will not set up any one with our Lord:

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: which guides to the right path; so we have believed in it and we will not associate anyone with our Lord,

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: 'It gives guidance to the Right, and we have believed therein: we shall not join (in worship) any (gods) with our Lord.

2.author: Transliteration
2.text: Yahdee ila alrrushdi faamanna bihi walan nushrika birabbina ahadan

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: Exalted is the glory of our Lord; He has neither wife nor son.

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: ‘And that our Lord’s Majesty is Supreme – He has neither chosen a wife nor a child.’

3.author: Arberry
3.text: He -- exalted be our Lord's majesty! has not taken to Himself either consort or a son.

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: And He - exalted be the majesty of our Lord!-hath taken neither a spouse nor a son.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: 'And exalted be the Majesty of our Lord, He has taken neither a wife, nor a son (or offspring or children).

3.author: Itani
3.text: And Exalted is the Grandeur of our Lord—He never had a mate, nor a child.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: and that “He – exalted be His Majesty – has not taken to Himself either a wife or a son”;

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: `And He, exalted be the Jadd of our Lord, has taken neither a wife nor a son.'

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: And (we believe) that He - exalted be the glory of our Lord! - hath taken neither wife nor son,

3.author: Qarai
3.text: Exalted be the majesty of our Lord; He has taken neither any spouse nor son.

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: He exalted be the Majesty of our Lord, who has neither taken to Himself a wife, nor a son!

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: And [it teaches] that exalted is the nobleness of our Lord; He has not taken a wife or a son

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: our Lord is too exalted to have either a wife or son.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: And that He-- exalted be the majesty of our Lord-- has not taken a consort, nor a son:

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: and exalted is the majesty of our Lord, He has taken neither a wife nor a son.

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: 'And Exalted is the Majesty of our Lord: He has taken neither a wife nor a son.

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: Waannahu taAAala jaddu rabbina ma ittakhatha sahibatan wala waladan

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: Certainly the foolish among us say preposterous things of God.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: ‘And that the fool among us used to utter false exaggerations against Allah.’

4.author: Arberry
4.text: The fool among us spoke against God outrage,

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: And the foolish among us were wont to forge lie against Allah exceedingly.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: 'And that the foolish among us [i.e. Iblis (Satan) or the polytheists amongst the jinns] used to utter against Allah that which was wrong and not right.

4.author: Itani
4.text: But the fools among us used to say nonsense about God.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: and that “the foolish among us have been wont to say outrageous things about Allah”;

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: `And that the foolish among us used to utter against Allah that which was an enormity in falsehood.'

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: And that the foolish one among us used to speak concerning Allah an atrocious lie.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: The foolish ones among us used to speak atrocious lies concerning Allah.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: The ignorant fool among us has spoken outrageously against Allah,

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: And that our foolish one has been saying about Allah an excessive transgression.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: The dimwit one (the devil) among us has been telling confused lies about God.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: And that the foolish amongst us used to forge extravagant things against Allah:

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: And [now we know] that the foolish among us have been saying outrageous things about God.

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: 'There were some foolish ones among us, who used to utter extravagant lies against Allah;

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: Waannahu kana yaqoolu safeehuna AAala Allahi shatatan

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: We had in fact thought that men and jinns would never speak a lie about God,

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: ‘Whereas we thought that men and jinns would never fabricate a lie against Allah!’

5.author: Arberry
5.text: and we had thought that men and jinn would never speak against God a lie.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: And verily we! we had imagined that humankind and Jinn would never forge against Allah a lie.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: 'And verily, we thought that men and jinns would not utter a lie against Allah.

5.author: Itani
5.text: And we thought that humans and jinn would never utter lies about God.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: and that “we had thought that men and jinn would never speak a lie about Allah”,

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: `And verily, we thought that men and Jinn would not utter a lie against Allah.'

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: And lo! we had supposed that humankind and jinn would not speak a lie concerning Allah -

5.author: Qarai
5.text: We thought that humans and jinn would never utter any falsehood concerning Allah.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: we never thought that either human or jinn would ever tell a lie against Allah!'

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: And we had thought that mankind and the jinn would never speak about Allah a lie.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: We thought that no man or jinn could ever tell lies about God.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: And that we thought that men and jinn did not utter a lie against Allah:

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: We had supposed that men and jinn would never utter a lie against God.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: 'But we do think that no man or spirit should say aught that untrue against Allah.

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Waanna thananna an lan taqoola alinsu waaljinnu AAala Allahi kathiban

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: But some men used to seek refuge with some jinns, and this increased their waywardness;

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: ‘And indeed some men among humans used to take the protection of some men among jinns, so it further increased their haughtiness.’

6.author: Arberry
6.text: But there were certain men of mankind who would take refuge with certain men of the jinn, and they increased them in vileness,

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: And persons among humankind have been seeking refuge with persons of the Jinn, so that they increased them in evil disposition.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: 'And verily, there were men among mankind who took shelter with the masculine among the jinns, but they (jinns) increased them (mankind) in sin and disbelief.

6.author: Itani
6.text: Some individual humans used to seek power through some individual jinn, but they only increased them in confusion.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: and that “some from among the humans used to seek protection of some among the jinn, and thus they increased the arrogance of the jinn”;

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: `And verily, there were men among mankind who took shelter with the males among the Jinn, but they increased them in Rahaq.'

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: And indeed (O Muhammad) individuals of humankind used to invoke the protection of individuals of the jinn, so that they increased them in revolt against Allah);

6.author: Qarai
6.text: Indeed some persons from the humans would seek the protection of some persons from the jinn, thus only adding to their rebellion.

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: But there were certain men from mankind who would take refuge with certain males from the jinn and they increased them in tyranny.

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: And there were men from mankind who sought refuge in men from the jinn, so they [only] increased them in burden.

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: "Certain human beings sought refuge with certain jinn and this increased the rebelliousness of those jinn.

6.author: Shakir
6.text: And that persons from among men used to seek refuge with persons from among jinn, so they increased them in wrongdoing:

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: Some men used to seek refuge with the jinn in the past, but that only increased their insolence.

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: 'True, there were persons among mankind who took shelter with persons among the Jinns, but they increased them in folly.

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Waannahu kana rijalun mina alinsi yaAAoothoona birijalin mina aljinni fazadoohum rahaqan

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: So they began to think, even as you do, that God would not resurrect any one.

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: ‘And that they assumed, like you humans assume, that Allah would not send any Noble Messenger.’

7.author: Arberry
7.text: and they thought, even as you also thought, that God would never raise up anyone.

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: And indeed they imagined, even as ye imagined, that Allah will not raise any one.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: 'And they thought as you thought, that Allah will not send any Messenger (to mankind or jinns).

7.author: Itani
7.text: They thought, as you thought, that God would never resurrect anyone.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: and that “they thought, even as you thought, that Allah would never raise anyone (as a Messenger)”;

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: `And they thought as you thought, that Allah will not send any Messenger.'

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: And indeed they supposed, even as ye suppose, that Allah would not raise anyone (from the dead) -

7.author: Qarai
7.text: They thought, just as you think, that Allah will not raise anyone from the dead.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: Like you, they thought that Allah would never raise the dead.

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: And they had thought, as you thought, that Allah would never send anyone [as a messenger].

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: Those people thought, like you, that God would never send down a Messenger.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: And that they thought as you think, that Allah would not raise anyone:

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: They thought, as you did, that God would never raise up anyone from the dead.

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: 'And they (came to) think as ye thought, that Allah would not raise up any one (to Judgment).

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Waannahum thannoo kama thanantum an lan yabAAatha Allahu ahadan

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: We sought to pry into the secrets of the heavens, but found it full of fierce guards and shooting flames.

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: ‘And we reached the sky, so we found it strongly guarded and filled with comets.’

8.author: Arberry
8.text: And we stretched towards heaven, but we found it filled with terrible guards and meteors.

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: And we sought to reach the heaven; then we found it filled with a strong guard and darting meteors.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: 'And we have sought to reach the heaven; but found it filled with stern guards and flaming fires.

8.author: Itani
8.text: We probed the heaven, and found it filled with stern guards and projectiles.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: and that “we tried to pry (the secrets of) the heaven, but we found it full of terrible guards and shooting meteors”;

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: `And we have sought to reach the heaven; but we found it filled with stern guards and flaming fires.'

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: And (the Jinn who had listened to the Qur'an said): We had sought the heaven but had found it filled with strong warders and meteors.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: Indeed we made for the heaven and found it full of mighty sentries and flames.

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: (The jinn continued saying): "We made our way towards heaven, but we found it filled with stern guards and a flame.

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: And we have sought [to reach] the heaven but found it filled with powerful guards and burning flames.

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: "We went near the heavens but found it to be full of strong guards and shooting flames.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: And that we sought to reach heaven, but we found it filled with strong guards and flaming stars.

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: We sought to reach heaven, but found it filled with strong guards and flames --

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: 'And we pried into the secrets of heaven; but we found it filled with stern guards and flaming fires.

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Waanna lamasna alssamaa fawajadnaha muliat harasan shadeedan washuhuban

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: We sat in observatories to listen; but any one who listened found a shooting star in wait for him.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: ‘And that we sometimes used to sit in some places in the sky, to listen; so whoever now listens finds a fiery comet waiting for him.’

9.author: Arberry
9.text: We would sit there on seats to hear; but any listening now finds a meteor in wait for him.

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: And we were wont to sit on seats therein to listen; but whosoever listeneth now findeth for him a dartin meteor in wait.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: 'And verily, we used to sit there in stations, to (steal) a hearing, but any who listens now will find a flaming fire watching him in ambush.

9.author: Itani
9.text: We used to take up positions to listen in; but whoever listens now finds a projectile in wait for him.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: and that “we would take up stations in the heaven to try to hear but anyone who now attempts to listen finds a shooting meteor in wait for him”;

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: `And verily, we used to sit there in stations, to (steal) a hearing, but any who listens now will find a flaming fire watching him in ambush.'

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: And we used to sit on places (high) therein to listen. But he who listeneth now findeth a flame in wait for him;

9.author: Qarai
9.text: We used to sit in its positions to eavesdrop, but anyone listening now finds a flame waiting for him.

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: There, we would sit to eavesdrop, but now an eavesdropper finds a flame in wait for him.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: And we used to sit therein in positions for hearing, but whoever listens now will find a burning flame lying in wait for him.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: We used to sit near by and try to listen to the heavens, but shooting flames now await those who try to do that.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: And that we used to sit in some of the sitting-places thereof to steal a hearing, but he who would (try to) listen now would find a flame lying in wait for him:

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: we used to take up a position to listen, but whoever listens now finds a flaming fire lying in wait for him --

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: 'We used, indeed, to sit there in (hidden) stations, to (steal) a hearing; but any who listen now will find a flaming fire watching him in ambush.

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Waanna kunna naqAAudu minha maqaAAida lilssamAAi faman yastamiAAi alana yajid lahu shihaban rasadan

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: We do not know if this means ill for the dwellers of the earth, or their Lord wishes guidance for them.

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: ‘And we do not know whether harm is intended for those on the earth, or whether their Lord intends goodness for them.’

10.author: Arberry
10.text: And so we know not whether evil is intended for those in the earth, or whether their Lord intends for them rectitude.

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: And we know not whether evil is boded for those who are on the earth, or whether their Lord intendeth for them a right direction.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: 'And we know not whether evil is intended for those on earth, or whether their Lord intends for them a Right Path.

10.author: Itani
10.text: We do not know whether ill is intended for those on earth, or if their Lord intends goodness for them.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: and that “we do not know whether evil is intended for those on the earth, or whether their Lord intends to direct them to the Right Way”;

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: `And we know not whether evil is intended for those on the earth, or whether their Lord intends for them guidance.'

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: And we know not whether harm is boded unto all who are in the earth, or whether their Lord intendeth guidance for them.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: We do not know whether ill is intended for those who are on the earth, or whether their Lord intends good for them.

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: And so we do not know whether evil is intended for those on earth, or whether their Lord intends to guide them.

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And we do not know [therefore] whether evil is intended for those on earth or whether their Lord intends for them a right course.

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: We do not know whether by this arrangement God intends benefit and guidance for the people of the earth or only evil.

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And that we know not whether evil is meant for those who are on earth or whether their Lord means to bring them good:

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: we cannot tell if this bodes evil to those who dwell on earth or whether their Lord intends to guide them.

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: 'And we understand not whether ill is intended to those on earth, or whether their Lord (really) intends to guide them to right conduct.

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Waanna la nadree asharrun oreeda biman fee alardi am arada bihim rabbuhum rashadan

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: For some of us are upright and some otherwise: Surely we follow different ways.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: ‘And among us some are virtuous and some are the other type; we are split into several branches.’

11.author: Arberry
11.text: And some of us are the righteous, and some of us are otherwise; we are sects differing.

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: And of us there are some righteous, and of us are some otherwise; we have been following very diverse.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: 'There are among us some that are righteous, and some the contrary; we are groups each having a different way (religious sect, etc.).

11.author: Itani
11.text: Some of us are righteous, but some of us are less than that; we follow divergent paths.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: and that “some of us are upright and some of us are otherwise for we follow widely divergent paths”;

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: `There are among us some that are righteous, and some the contrary; we are groups having different ways.'

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: And among us there are righteous folk and among us there are far from that. We are sects having different rules.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: Among us some are righteous and some are otherwise: we are various sects.

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: Some of us are righteous, but some are otherwise, we are sects that differ.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: And among us are the righteous, and among us are [others] not so; we were [of] divided ways.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: As for us, some of us are righteous and others are not. We have all followed different ways.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: And that some of us are good and others of us are below that: we are sects following different ways:

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: Some of us are righteous, while others are not; we follow divergent paths.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: 'There are among us some that are righteous, and some the contrary: we follow divergent paths.

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Waanna minna alssalihoona waminna doona thalika kunna taraiqa qidadan

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: We realised that we could not weaken the power of God on earth, nor outpace Him by running away.

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: ‘And we are certain that we cannot defeat Allah in the earth, nor can we run out of His grasp.’

12.author: Arberry
12.text: Indeed, we thought that we should never be able to frustrate God in the earth, neither be able to frustrate Him by flight.

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: And we known that we cannot frustrate Allah in the earth, nor can we frustrate Him by flight.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: 'And we think that we cannot escape (from the punishment of) Allah in the earth, nor can we escape (from the punishment) by flight.

12.author: Itani
12.text: We realized that we cannot defeat God on earth, and that we cannot escape Him by fleeing.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: and that “we thought that we will neither be able to frustrate Allah on earth, nor frustrate Him by flight”;

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: `And we think that we cannot escape Allah in the earth, nor can we escape Him by flight.'

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: And we know that we cannot escape from Allah in the earth, nor can we escape by flight.

12.author: Qarai
12.text: We know that we cannot frustrate Allah on the earth, nor can we frustrate Him by fleeing.

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: We know that we cannot frustrate Allah in the earth, nor can we frustrate Him by flight.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: And we have become certain that we will never cause failure to Allah upon earth, nor can we escape Him by flight.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: We knew that we could never challenge God whether we stayed on earth or fled elsewhere.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: And that we know that we cannot escape Allah in the earth, nor can we escape Him by flight:

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: We have realized that we could never thwart God on earth and that we would never be able to thwart Him by flight.

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: 'But we think that we can by no means frustrate Allah throughout the earth, nor can we frustrate Him by flight.

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: Wanna thananna an lan nuAAjiza Allaha fee alardi walan nuAAjizahu haraban

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: So when we heard the guidance we believed in it; and he who believes in his Lord will neither fear loss nor force.

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: ‘And that when we heard the guidance, we accepted faith in it; so whoever accepts faith in his Lord, has no fear – neither of any loss nor of any injustice.’

13.author: Arberry
13.text: When we heard the guidance, we believed in it; and whosoever believes in his Lord, he shall fear neither paltriness nor vileness.

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And when we heard the Message of guidance, we believed therein; and whosoever believeth in his Lord, he shall fear neither diminution nor wrong.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: 'And indeed when we heard the Guidance (this Quran), we believed therein (Islamic Monotheism), and whosoever believes in his Lord shall have no fear, either of a decrease in the reward of his good deeds or an increase in punishment for his sins.

13.author: Itani
13.text: And when we heard the guidance, we believed in it. Whoever believes in his Lord fears neither loss, nor burden.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: and that “when we heard the teaching of the Right Way we came to believe in it; he who believes in His Lord shall have no fear of suffering loss or being subjected to any injustice”;

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: `And indeed when we heard the Guidance, we believed therein, and whosoever believes in his Lord shall have no fear, either of a decrease in the reward of his good deeds or an increase in the punishment for his sins.'

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: And when we heard the guidance, we believed therein, and whoso believeth in his Lord, he feareth neither loss nor oppression.

13.author: Qarai
13.text: When we heard the [message of] guidance, we believed in it. Whoever that has faith in his Lord will fear neither privation nor oppression.

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: When we heard the Guidance, we believed in it, and whosoever believes in his Lord shall fear neither shortage nor injustice.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: And when we heard the guidance, we believed in it. And whoever believes in his Lord will not fear deprivation or burden.

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: Now that we have listened to the guidance, we believe in it. Whoever believes in his Lord does not need to fear loss or oppression.

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And that when we heard the guidance, we believed in it; so whoever believes in his Lord, he should neither fear loss nor being overtaken (by disgrace):

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: When we heard the call to guidance, we believed in it. He who believes in his Lord has no fear of loss or of injustice.

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: 'And as for us, since we have listened to the Guidance, we have accepted it: and any who believes in his Lord has no fear, either of a short (account) or of any injustice.

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: Waanna lamma samiAAna alhuda amanna bihi faman yumin birabbihi fala yakhafu bakhsan wala rahaqan

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: Some of us have come to submission, and some of us are iniquitous.'" Those who have submitted have taken the right course;

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: ‘And that some among us are Muslims and some are the unjust; and whoever has accepted Islam – it is they who have thought rightly.’

14.author: Arberry
14.text: And some of us have surrendered, and some of us have deviated. Those who have surrendered sought rectitude;

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And of us some are Muslims, and of us some are deviators. Then whosoever hath embraced Islam - such have endeavoured after a Path of rectitude.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: 'And of us some are Muslims (who have submitted to Allah, after listening to this Quran), and of us some are Al-Qasitun (disbelievers those who have deviated from the Right Path)'. And whosoever has embraced Islam (i.e. has become a Muslim by submitting to Allah), then such have sought the Right Path."

14.author: Itani
14.text: Among us are those who are submitting, and among us are the compromisers. As for those who have submitted—it is they who pursue rectitude.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: and that “among us some are Muslims (Those who have submitted to Allah), and some of us are deviant. So those who became Muslims found the Right Course;

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: `And of us some are Muslims, and of us some are Al-Qasitun. And whosoever has embraced Islam, then such have sought the right path.' "

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And there are among us some who have surrendered (to Allah) and there are among us some who are unjust. And whoso hath surrendered to Allah, such have taken the right path purposefully.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: Among us some are Muslims and some of us are perverse.” ’ Those who submit [to Allah]—it is they who pursue rectitude.

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: Some of us have surrendered (Muslims) and some of us have deviated. Those who surrendered sought the Right Path,

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And among us are Muslims [in submission to Allah], and among us are the unjust. And whoever has become Muslim - those have sought out the right course.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: Some of us are Muslims and some of us have deviated from the Truth. Whoever has embraced Islam has followed the right guidance.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And that some of us are those who submit, and some of us are the deviators; so whoever submits, these aim at the right way:

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: Some of us are obedient while others are wrongdoers; it is the obedient who have found the right path,

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: 'Amongst us are some that submit their wills (to Allah), and some that swerve from justice. Now those who submit their wills - they have sought out (the path) of right conduct:

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Waanna minna almuslimoona waminna alqasitoona faman aslama faolaika taharraw rashadan

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: But those who are iniquitous will be fuel for Hell.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: ‘And as for the unjust – they are the fuel of hell.’”

15.author: Arberry
15.text: but as for those who have deviated, they have become firewood for Gehenna!"'

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: And as for the deviators, for Hell they shall be fuel.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: And as for the Qasitun (disbelievers who deviated from the Right Path), they shall be firewood for Hell,

15.author: Itani
15.text: But as for the compromisers—they will be firewood for Hell.’”

15.author: Maududi
15.text: but those who deviated from the Truth, will be the fuel for Hell.”

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: And as for the Qasitun, they shall be firewood for Hell.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: And as for those who are unjust, they are firewood for hell.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: As for the perverse, they will be firewood for hell.

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: but those who have deviated shall become the fuel of Gehenna (Hell).

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: But as for the unjust, they will be, for Hell, firewood.'

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: However, the deviators from the Truth will be the fuel for hell".

15.author: Shakir
15.text: And as to the deviators, they are fuel of hell:

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: but those who are wrongdoers will become the fuel of Hell."

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: 'But those who swerve,- they are (but) fuel for Hell-fire'-

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: (Say): "If they keep to the right path We shall give them water in abundance to drink

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: And proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “I have received the divine revelation that ‘Had they remained upright on the straight path, We would have given them abundant water.’

16.author: Arberry
16.text: Would they but go straight on the way, We would give them to drink of water copious,

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: And had they kept to the path surely We would have watered them with rain plenteous.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: If they (non-Muslims) had believed in Allah, and went on the Right Way (i.e. Islam) We should surely have bestowed on them water (rain) in abundance.

16.author: Itani
16.text: Had they kept true to the Path, We would have given them plenty water to drink.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: If people were to keep firmly to the Right Way, We would have vouchsafed them abundant rain

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: If they had believed in Allah, and went on the way, We would surely have bestowed on them water in abundance.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: If they (the idolaters) tread the right path, We shall give them to drink of water in abundance

16.author: Qarai
16.text: If they are steadfast on the path [of Allah], We shall provide them with abundant water,

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: If they had followed the Path We would give to them abundant water to drink,

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: And [Allah revealed] that if they had remained straight on the way, We would have given them abundant provision

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: Had they (jinn and mankind) remained steadfast in their religion (Islam), We would certainly have given them abundant water to drink

16.author: Shakir
16.text: And that if they should keep to the (right) way, We would certainly give them to drink of abundant water,

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: If they had followed the right path, We would have provided them with abundant rain --

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: (And Allah's Message is): "If they (the Pagans) had (only) remained on the (right) Way, We should certainly have bestowed on them Rain in abundance.

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Waallawi istaqamoo AAala alttareeqati laasqaynahum maan ghadaqan

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: In order to try them through it. But whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord, will be given increasing torment by Him."

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: ‘In order to test them with it; and whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord – He will put him in a punishment that keeps on increasing.’

17.author: Arberry
17.text: that We might try them therein. And whosoever turns away from the Remembrance of his Lord, He will thrust him into chastisement rigorous.

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: That We might try them there by. And whosoever turneth aside from the remembrance of his Lord, him He shall thrust into a torment vehement.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: That We might try them thereby. And whosoever turns away from the Reminder of his Lord (i.e. this Quran, and practice not its laws and orders), He will cause him to enter in a severe torment (i.e. Hell).

17.author: Itani
17.text: To test them with it. Whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord, He will direct him to torment ever mounting.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: so that We might try them through this bounty. Whoso turns away from the remembrance of his Lord, He will cause him to suffer a grievous chastisement;

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: That We might try them thereby. And whosoever turns away from the Reminder of his Lord, He will cause him to enter in a Sa`ad torment.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: That We may test them thereby, and whoso turneth away from the remembrance of his Lord; He will thrust him into ever-growing torment.

17.author: Qarai
17.text: so that We may test them therein, and whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord, He will let him into an escalating punishment.

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: and tested them with it. And whosoever turns away from his Lord's Remembrance, He will hurl him into a stern punishment.

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: So We might test them therein. And whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord He will put into arduous punishment.

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: as a trial for them. God will make those who disregard the guidance from their Lord suffer increasing torment.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: So that We might try them with respect to it; and whoever turns aside from the reminder of his Lord, He will make him enter into an afflicting chastisement:

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: so that We may test them by it -- whoever turns away from the remembrance of his Lord shall be sternly punished.

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: "That We might try them by that (means). But if any turns away from the remembrance of his Lord, He will cause him to undergo a severe Penalty.

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Linaftinahum feehi waman yuAArid AAan thikri rabbihi yaslukhu AAathaban saAAadan

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: All places of worship are for God; so do not invoke any one with God.

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: ‘And that the mosques are for Allah only – therefore do not worship anyone along with Allah.’

18.author: Arberry
18.text: The places of worship belong to God; so call not, along with God, upon anyone.

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: And the prostrations are for Allah; wherefore call not along with Allah anyone.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: And the mosques are for Allah (Alone), so invoke not anyone along with Allah.

18.author: Itani
18.text: The places of worship are for God. So do not call, besides God, upon anyone else.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: and that “mosques belong to Allah, so do not invoke anyone with Him”;

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: And the Masjids are for Allah, so invoke not anyone along with Allah.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: And the places of worship are only for Allah, so pray not unto anyone along with Allah.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: The places of worship belong to Allah, so do not invoke anyone along with Allah.

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: Mosques belong to Allah, so do not call to anyone else, other than Allah.

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: And [He revealed] that the masjids are for Allah, so do not invoke with Allah anyone.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: All the parts of the body to be placed on the ground during prostration belong to God.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: And that the mosques are Allah's, therefore call not upon any one with Allah:

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: The mosques are for God's worship -- so do not invoke anyone else along with God --

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: "And the places of worship are for Allah (alone): So invoke not any one along with Allah;

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Waanna almasajida lillahi fala tadAAoo maAAa Allahi ahadan

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: When the devotee of God stood up to invoke Him (the jinns) crowded upon him (to listen).

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: ‘And that when Allah’s bondman stood up to worship Him, the jinns had almost crowded upon him.’”

19.author: Arberry
19.text: When the servant of God stood calling on Him, they were wellnigh upon him in swarms.

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: And when the bondman of Allah stood calling upon Him, they well nigh pressed on him stifling.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: (It has been revealed to me that) When the slave of Allah (Muhammad SAW) stood up invoking (his Lord Allah) in prayer to Him they (the jinns) just made round him a dense crowd as if sticking one over the other (in order to listen to the Prophet's recitation).

19.author: Itani
19.text: And when the servant of God got up calling on Him, they almost fell on him in a mass.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: and when Allah's servant stood up to call on Him, they well-nigh swarmed him.

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: And when the servant of Allah stood up invoking Him in prayer they just made round him a dense crowd as if sticking one over the other.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: And when the slave of Allah stood up in prayer to Him, they crowded on him, almost stifling.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: When the servant of Allah rose to pray to Him, they almost crowded around him.

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: When the worshiper of Allah stood supplicating to Him, they swarmed around him (Prophet Muhammad).

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: And that when the Servant of Allah stood up supplicating Him, they almost became about him a compacted mass."

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: Do not prostrate before anyone other than Him. When the servant of God (Muhammad) preached (his message) the jinn would all crowd around him.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: And that when the servant of Allah stood up calling upon Him, they wellnigh crowded him (to death).

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: when God's servant stood up to pray to Him, they pressed close to him in great numbers, almost stifling him.

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: "Yet when the Devotee of Allah stands forth to invoke Him, they just make round him a dense crowd."

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Waannahu lamma qama AAabdu Allahi yadAAoohu kadoo yakoonoona AAalayhi libadan

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: Say: "I call on my Lord alone and I do not associate any one with Him."

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: Say (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “I worship only Allah, and I do not ascribe any partner to Him.”

20.author: Arberry
20.text: Say: 'I call only upon my Lord, and I do not associate with Him anyone.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: Say thou: I simply call upon Allah, and I associate not with Him any-one.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "I invoke only my Lord (Allah Alone), and I associate none as partners along with Him."

20.author: Itani
20.text: Say, “I pray only to my Lord, and I never associate anyone with Him.”

20.author: Maududi
20.text: Say, (O Prophet): “I call on my Lord alone, and I do not associate aught with Him in His Divinity.”

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: Say: "I invoke only my Lord, and I associate none as partners along with Him."

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: Say (unto them, O Muhammad): I pray unto Allah only, and ascribe unto Him no partner.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: Say, ‘I pray only to my Lord, and I do not ascribe any partner to Him.’

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: Say: 'I supplicate only to my Lord and I do not associate any with Him'

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: Say, [O Muhammad], "I only invoke my Lord and do not associate with Him anyone."

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: (Muhammad), say, "I worship only my Lord and do not consider anyone equal to Him".

20.author: Shakir
20.text: Say: I only call upon my Lord, and I do not associate any one with Him.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: Say, "I call only upon my Lord and do not associate anyone else with Him."

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: Say: "I do no more than invoke my Lord, and I join not with Him any (false god)."

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: Qul innama adAAoo rabbee wala oshriku bihi ahadan

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: Say: "Neither is your loss within my power nor bringing you to guidance."

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “I am not the master of any harm or benefit for you.”

21.author: Arberry
21.text: Say: 'Surely I possess no power over you, either for hurt or for rectitude.'

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: Say thou: 'verily own not for you power of hurt nor of benefit

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: Say: "It is not in my power to cause you harm, or to bring you to the Right Path."

21.author: Itani
21.text: Say, “It is not in my power to harm you, nor to bring you to right conduct.”

21.author: Maududi
21.text: Say: “Surely neither it is in my power to hurt you nor to bring you to the Right Way.”

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: Say: "It is not in my power to cause you harm, or to bring you to the right path."

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: Say: Lo! I control not hurt nor benefit for you.

21.author: Qarai
21.text: Say, ‘I have no power to bring you any harm or good [of my own accord].’

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: Say: 'Indeed, I possess no power over you, either for harm or for guidance'

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: Say, "Indeed, I do not possess for you [the power of] harm or right direction."

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: Say, "I do not possess any power to harm or benefit you".

21.author: Shakir
21.text: Say: I do not control for you evil or good.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: Say, "It is not in my power to cause you either harm or good?"

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: Say: "It is not in my power to cause you harm, or to bring you to right conduct."

21.author: Transliteration
21.text: Qul innee la amliku lakum darran wala rashadan

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: Say: "No one can save me from God, nor can I find a place of refuge apart from Him,

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: Say, “No one will ever save me from Allah, and other than Him, I will not find any refuge.”

22.author: Arberry
22.text: Say: 'From God shall protect me not anyone, and I shall find, apart from Him, no refuge,

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: Say thou: 'verily none can protect me from Allah, nor can I find beside Him any refuge.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "None can protect me from Allah's punishment (if I were to disobey Him), nor should I find refuge except in Him.

22.author: Itani
22.text: Say, “No one can protect me from God, and I will not find any refuge except with Him.

22.author: Maududi
22.text: Say: “None can protect me from Allah, nor can I find a refuge apart from Him.

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: Say: "None can protect me from Allah's punishment, nor can I find refuge except in Him."

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: Say: Lo! none can protect me from Allah, nor can I find any refuge beside Him

22.author: Qarai
22.text: Say, ‘Neither can anyone shelter me from Allah, nor can I find any refuge besides Him.

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: Say: 'None shall protect me from Allah, and other than Him, I will find no refuge,

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: Say, "Indeed, there will never protect me from Allah anyone [if I should disobey], nor will I find in other than Him a refuge.

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: Say, "No one can protect me from God, nor can I find any place of refuge but with him.

22.author: Shakir
22.text: Say: Surely no one can protect me against Allah, nor can I find besides Him any place of refuge:

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: Say, "Surely no one can protect me against God, nor can I find besides Him any place of refuge.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: Say: "No one can deliver me from Allah (If I were to disobey Him), nor should I find refuge except in Him,

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Qul innee lan yujeeranee mina Allahi ahadun walan ajida min doonihi multahadan

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: Unless I convey from God and deliver His message." For those who disobey God and His Apostle is the fire of Hell, where they will abide for ever;

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: “I only convey the commands from Allah, and His messages; and whoever disobeys the commands of Allah and His Noble Messenger – then indeed for him is the fire of hell, in which they will remain for ever and ever.”

23.author: Arberry
23.text: excepting a Deliverance from God and His Messages. And whoso rebels against God and His messenger, for him there awaits the Fire of Gehenna; therein they shall dwell forever.'

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: Mine is but the publishing from Allah and His messages; and whosoever disobeyeth Allah and His apostle, his portion verily is the Hell-Fire as abiders therein for evermore.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: "(Mine is) but conveyance (of the truth) from Allah and His Messages (of Islamic Monotheism), and whosoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger, then verily, for him is the Fire of Hell, he shall dwell therein forever."

23.author: Itani
23.text: Except for a proclamation from God and His messages. He who defies God and His Messenger—for him is the Fire of Hell, in which they will dwell forever.”

23.author: Maududi
23.text: (My task is no more than) to deliver Allah's proclamation and His messages. And whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger, surely the Fire of Hell awaits him; therein he will abide in perpetuity.”

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: "(Mine is) but conveyance from Allah and His Messages, and whosoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger, then verily, for him is the fire of Hell, he shall dwell therein forever."

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: (Mine is) but conveyance (of the Truth) from Allah, and His messages; and whoso disobeyeth Allah and His messenger, lo! his is fire of hell, wherein such dwell for ever.

23.author: Qarai
23.text: [I have no duty] except to transmit from Allah, and [to communicate] His messages; and whoever disobeys Allah and His apostle, there will indeed be for him the fire of hell, to remain in it forever.’

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: except a deliverance from Allah and His Messages. He who disobeys Allah and His Messenger, for him is the Fire of Gehenna, which he will be in for ever.

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: But [I have for you] only notification from Allah, and His messages." And whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger - then indeed, for him is the fire of Hell; they will abide therein forever.

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: My only (means of protection) is to convey the message of God. Whoever disobeys God and His Messenger will go to hell, wherein he will live forever".

23.author: Shakir
23.text: (It is) only a delivering (of communications) from Allah and His messages; and whoever disobeys Allah and His Apostle surely he shall have the fire of hell to abide therein for a long time.

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: My duty is only to convey that which I receive from Him and His messages." For those who disobey God and His Messenger there is the fire of hell, wherein they will abide forever.

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: "Unless I proclaim what I receive from Allah and His Messages: for any that disobey Allah and His Messenger,- for them is Hell: they shall dwell therein for ever."

23.author: Transliteration
23.text: Illa balaghan mina Allahi warisalatihi waman yaAAsi Allaha warasoolahu fainna lahu nara jahannama khalideena feeha abadan

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: Until they see what they are promised, when they will understand who is weaker in aid and poorer in numbers.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: Till the time when they will see what they are promised – so they will now come to know whose aide is weak, and who is lesser in number.

24.author: Arberry
24.text: Until, when they see that which they are promised, then they will know who is weaker in helpers and fewer in numbers.

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: They shall go on denying until they; behold that which they are promised wherefore then they shall know who were weaker in protectors and fewer in number.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: Till, when they see that which they are promised, then they will know who it is that is weaker concerning helpers and less important concerning numbers.

24.author: Itani
24.text: Until, when they see what they were promised, they will know who is weaker in helpers, and fewer in numbers.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: (They shall not change their ways) until they see that against which they had been warned, and then they will know whose helpers are weaker and whose supporters are fewer in number.

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: Till, when they see that which they are promised, then they will know who it is that is weaker concerning helpers and less important concerning numbers.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: Till (the day) when they shall behold that which they are promised (they may doubt); but then they will know (for certain) who is weaker in allies and less in multitude.

24.author: Qarai
24.text: When they see what they are promised, they will know who is weaker in supporters and fewer in numbers.

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: When they see that which they were promised they shall know who is weaker in helpers and fewer in numbers.

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: [The disbelievers continue] until, when they see that which they are promised, then they will know who is weaker in helpers and less in number.

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: (On the Day of Judgment) when the disbelievers witness that with which they have been threatened, they will then know whose helpers are weaker and fewer in number.

24.author: Shakir
24.text: Until when they see what they are threatened with, then shall they know who is weaker in helpers and fewer in number.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: When they are confronted by what they have been promised, they will realize who is weaker in helpers and fewer in numbers.

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: At length, when they see (with their own eyes) that which they are promised,- then will they know who it is that is weakest in (his) helper and least important in point of numbers.

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Hatta itha raaw ma yooAAadoona fasayaAAlamoona man adAAafu nasiran waaqallu AAadadan

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: Say: "I do not know if what is promised you is near, or if my Lord prolongs its term.

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: Say (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “I do not know whether what you are promised is close or if my Lord will postpone it for a while.”

25.author: Arberry
25.text: Say: 'I do not know whether that which you are promised is nigh, or whether my Lord will appoint for it a space;

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: Say thou: know not whather that which ye are promised is nigh, or whether my Lord hath appointed for it a distant term.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "I know not whether (the punishment) which you are promised is near or whether my Lord will appoint for it a distant term.

25.author: Itani
25.text: Say, “I do not know whether what you are promised is near, or whether my Lord will extend it for a period.”

25.author: Maududi
25.text: Say: “I know not whether what you are promised is near or whether my Lord will prolong its term.

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: Say: "I know not whether that which you are promised is near or whether my Lord will appoint for it a distant term."

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: Say (O Muhammad, unto the disbelievers): I know not whether that which ye are promised is nigh, or if my Lord hath set a distant term for it.

25.author: Qarai
25.text: Say, ‘I do not know if what you are promised is near, or if my Lord has set a [long] term for it.’

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: Say: 'I do not know whether that which you are promised is near, or whether my Lord will set for it a time.

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: Say, "I do not know if what you are promised is near or if my Lord will grant for it a [long] period."

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: (Muhammad), say, "I do not know whether that with which you have been threatened is close by or whether my Lord will prolong the time of its coming.

25.author: Shakir
25.text: Say: I do not know whether that with which you are threatened be nigh or whether my Lord will appoint for it a term:

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: Say, "I do not know whether what you are promised is imminent, or whether my Lord has set for it a far-off day.

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: Say: "I know not whether the (Punishment) which ye are promised is near, or whether my Lord will appoint for it a distant term.

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Qul in adree aqareebun ma tooAAadoona am yajAAalu lahu rabbee amadan

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: He is the knower of the Unknown, and He does not divulge His secret to any one

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: The All Knowing of all the hidden does not give anyone the control over His secrets.

26.author: Arberry
26.text: Knower He of the Unseen, and He discloses not His Unseen to anyone,

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: He is the Knower of the unseen, and He discloseth not His unseen Unto anyone.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: "(He Alone) the All-Knower of the Gha'ib (unseen), and He reveals to none His Gha'ib (unseen)."

26.author: Itani
26.text: The Knower of the Invisible; He does not disclose His Invisible to anyone.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: He is the Knower of the Unseen, and He does not disclose His Unseen to anyone

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: "The All-Knower of the Unseen, and He reveals to none His Unseen."

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: (He is) the Knower of the Unseen, and He revealeth unto none His secret,

26.author: Qarai
26.text: Knower of the Unseen, He does not disclose His [knowledge of the] Unseen to anyone,

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: He is the Knower of the Unseen and does not disclose His Unseen to anyone,

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: [He is] Knower of the unseen, and He does not disclose His [knowledge of the] unseen to anyone

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: He knows the unseen and He does not allow anyone to know His secrets except those of His Messengers whom He chooses.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: The Knower of the unseen! so He does not reveal His secrets to any,

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: He alone has knowledge of what is hidden. He reveals this to none,

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: "He (alone) knows the Unseen, nor does He make any one acquainted with His Mysteries,-

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: AAalimu alghaybi fala yuthhiru AAala ghaybihi ahadan

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: Other than an apostle He has chosen, when He makes a sentinel walk in front of him and a sentinel behind,

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: Except to His chosen Noble Messengers – so He appoints guards in front and behind him. (Allah gave the knowledge of the hidden to the Holy Prophet – peace and blessings be upon him.)

27.author: Arberry
27.text: save only to such a Messenger as He is well-pleased with; then He despatches before him and behind him watchers,

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: Save Unto an apostle chosen. And then He causeth to go before him and behind him a guard.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: Except to a Messenger (from mankind) whom He has chosen (He informs him of unseen as much as He likes), and then He makes a band of watching guards (angels) to march before him and behind him.

27.author: Itani
27.text: Except to a Messenger of His choosing. He then dispatches guards before him and behind him.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: other than to a Messenger whom He chooses (for the bestowal of any part of the knowledge of the Unseen), whereafter He appoints guards who go before him and behind him,

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: Except to a Messenger whom He has chosen, and then He makes a band of watching guards to march before him and behind him.

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: Save unto every messenger whom He hath chosen, and then He maketh a guard to go before him and a guard behind him

27.author: Qarai
27.text: except to an apostle He approves of. Then He dispatches a sentinel before and behind him

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: except only to a Messenger that He has chosen, and He sends guardians before him and behind him,

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: Except whom He has approved of messengers, and indeed, He sends before each messenger and behind him observers

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: He causes angelic guards to march before and after him.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: Except to him whom He chooses as an apostle; for surely He makes a guard to march before him and after him,

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: except the messenger whom He has chosen. He sends down guardians who walk before them and behind them,

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: "Except a messenger whom He has chosen: and then He makes a band of watchers march before him and behind him,

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Illa mani irtada min rasoolin fainnahu yasluku min bayni yadayhi wamin khalfihi rasadan

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: So that He may know if they have delivered their Lord's messages. He comprehends all that has been given them, and keeps count of everything.

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: In order to see that they have conveyed the messages of their Lord – and His knowledge encompasses all whatever they have, and He has kept all things accounted for.

28.author: Arberry
28.text: that He may know they have delivered the Messages of their Lord; and He encompasses all that is with them, and He has numbered everything in numbers.'

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: That He may know that they have preached the message of their Lord. And He comprehendeth whatever is with them, and He keepeth count of everything numbered.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: [He (Allah) protects them (the Messengers)], till He sees that they (the Messengers) have conveyed the Messages of their Lord (Allah). And He (Allah) surrounds all that which is with them, and He (Allah) keeps count of all things (i.e. He knows the exact number of everything).

28.author: Itani
28.text: That He may know that they have conveyed the messages of their Lord. He encompasses what they have, and has tallied everything by number.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: so that He may know that they have delivered the messages of their Lord. He encompasses in His knowledge their surroundings and keeps a count of all things.”

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: Till he knows that they have conveyed the Messages of their Lord. And He surrounds all that which is with them, and He keeps count of all things.

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: That He may know that they have indeed conveyed the messages of their Lord. He surroundeth all their doings, and He keepeth count of all things.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: so that He may ascertain that they have communicated the messages of their Lord, and He encompasses all that is with them, and He keeps an count of all things.

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: so that He may know that they have delivered the Messages of their Lord and He encompasses all that is with them, and He has counted everything innumbers'

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: That he may know that they have conveyed the messages of their Lord; and He has encompassed whatever is with them and has enumerated all things in number.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: (Messenger) so that He would know that the Messengers have conveyed the message of their Lord. He encompasses all that is with them and He keeps a precise account of all things".

28.author: Shakir
28.text: So that He may know that they have truly delivered the messages of their Lord, and He encompasses what is with them and He records the number of all things.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: so that He may know that the messengers have delivered the messages of their Lord. He encompasses all that is with them and He keeps count of all things.

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: "That He may know that they have (truly) brought and delivered the Messages of their Lord: and He surrounds (all the mysteries) that are with them, and takes account of every single thing."

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: LiyaAAlama an qad ablaghoo risalati rabbihim waahata bima ladayhim waahsa kulla shayin AAadadan
